meta.title: Morteza Pourmirzai | Where Nature Meets Code
meta.description: Morteza Pourmirzai — conservationist with 17+ years on the frontline of Asiatic cheetah rescue, and a web developer. Where Nature Meets Code.

about.title: Two Worlds, One Person
about.bio: For over 17 years I have stood on the frontline of Asiatic cheetah conservation — the last of the species in Iran and the world — while building the web and architecting systems alongside it. From wildlife refuges to lines of code, my mission is one: building a more sustainable future.
about.bio2: CEO & Co-Founder of Sarvin Wildlife Conservation, former CEO of the Iranian Cheetah Society, and an open-source developer on GitHub.

timeline.text: Began environmental studies and volunteer conservation work
Field projects with the Iranian Cheetah Society in Touran & Miandasht
Became CEO of the Iranian Cheetah Society
Presented at the Asia Conservation Congress, Nepal

worlds.title: Conservation & Technology
worlds.subtitle: Two domains that seem far apart, yet complete each other in practice.

nature.label: Conservation World
nature.title: Saving the Asiatic Cheetah
nature.desc: Fieldwork to save the world's last Asiatic cheetahs — focused on habitat protection, camera traps and ranger training.
achievements: Partnered with IUCN NL & World Land Trust

tech.label: Tech World
tech.title: Building Web & Automation
tech.desc: Building tools, games and automations that serve both conservation and clients.

featured.title: Real Impact
featured.subtitle: Projects where nature and code meet.

press.title: Recognized Globally
press.subtitle: The voice of Asiatic cheetah conservation in international media.
press.quote: Iran's Asiatic cheetahs face an 'extremely' precarious risk of extinction.
